ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Shish Tawook Wrap; FOODON_00002473 (wrap sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, grilled — used as the primary protein anchor for this composite wrap dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a composite Shish Tawook Wrap entry; A0FJP (grilled chicken) is used as the dominant ingredient anchor. The flatbread and vegetable components are not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for Shish Tawook Wrap or a sufficiently close composite wrap dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Shish Tawook Wrap weighs approximately 350 g and constitutes a complete single-meal serving as sold by food delivery vendors in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, cooked, roasted (FDC ID 171477); Bread, pita, white, enriched (FDC ID 174924); Tomatoes, red, ripe, raw (FDC ID 170457); Cucumber, with peel, raw (FDC ID 168409); Yogurt, plain, whole milk (FDC ID 171284)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Shish Tawook Wrap as a composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted-component method using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for grilled chicken breast (FDC ID 171477), white pita bread (FDC ID 174924), raw tomato (FDC ID 170457), raw cucumber (FDC ID 168409), and plain whole-milk yogurt (FDC ID 171284). Estimated component proportions per 100 g of wrap: grilled chicken ~38 g, pita bread ~28 g, tomato ~10 g, cucumber ~8 g, yogurt-garlic sauce ~12 g, olive oil marinade ~4 g. The input record values (GrabFood provenance, confidence 0.7) were cross-referenced against these USDA component calculations and retained where they fell within plausible ranges. Energy (165.7 kcal/100g), protein (10.9 g), fat (8.0 g), carbohydrate (12.9 g), and sodium (314 mg/100g) are all consistent with the USDA component-weighted estimates for this dish type.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Pita bread, white (GI 57, glucose reference)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Shish Tawook Wrap as a composite dish. GI estimated at 57 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being white pita bread (GI 57, glucose reference scale), as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of GI and GL Values 2008 (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). The protein (10.9 g/100g) and fat (8.0 g/100g) content of the wrap will attenuate the glycaemic response relative to plain pita consumed alone. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (57) × available carbohydrate per serving (45.0 g, derived from 350 g × 12.86 g/100g) ÷ 100 = 25.7, rounded to 25.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 314 mg per 100 g; a full 350 g wrap delivers approximately 1,100 mg sodium, representing 48–55% of the recommended daily limit of 2,000–2,300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced sauce and no pickles to lower sodium intake, limit consumption to one wrap per meal, and pair with potassium-rich foods such as banana or leafy greens to support sodium-potassium balance consistent with DASH dietary principles.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated GI of 57 (medium) driven by white pita flatbread; however, the 10.9 g protein and 8.0 g fat per 100 g attenuate postprandial glucose rise, and sugar content is low at 1.43 g per 100 g.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can include this wrap in a balanced meal plan by requesting wholemeal or high-fibre pita to lower the glycaemic index, moderating portion to half a wrap if carbohydrate budget is restricted, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables to further blunt the glycaemic load of approximately 25.8 per full serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is moderate at 2.0 g per 100 g (7.0 g per full serving); sodium is elevated at 314 mg per 100 g; however, the lean grilled chicken protein and olive oil-based marinade provide a favourable unsaturated fat profile relative to red-meat alternatives.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, choose this wrap over red-meat kebab options, request sauce on the side to reduce saturated fat and sodium, limit to one serving per meal, and ensure the overall daily diet remains within the AHA-recommended limit of less than 13 g saturated fat and 1,500–2,000 mg sodium per day.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Grilled chicken breast is low in arachidonic acid; olive oil marinade provides anti-inflammatory monounsaturated fatty acids and polyphenols; yogurt contributes calcium and live cultures.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis may consume this wrap but should monitor individual response to nightshade ingredients (tomato, paprika), request reduced pickles to minimise sodium-driven tissue swelling, and substitute mayonnaise-based sauce with olive oil-based dressing to avoid pro-inflammatory refined seed oils.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shish Tawook Wrap is a Turkish-origin grilled chicken wrap widely served in Singapore through food delivery platforms and Middle Eastern casual dining outlets. The dish consists of boneless chicken pieces — typically breast or thigh — marinated in a blend of yogurt, lemon juice, garlic, olive oil, cumin, paprika, and oregano, then grilled over high heat until lightly charred and juicy. The grilled chicken is placed inside a soft flatbread (pita or lavash), accompanied by fresh tomato, cucumber, lettuce, and a creamy garlic-yogurt or toum sauce, and sometimes pickled vegetables. The flavour profile is savoury, mildly tangy from the yogurt marinade, with aromatic spice notes and a slight smokiness from grilling. The texture contrasts tender, moist chicken with the soft chew of flatbread and the crunch of fresh vegetables. Nutritionally, a 100 g portion provides approximately 165.7 kcal, 10.9 g protein, 8.0 g fat (2.0 g saturated), and 12.9 g carbohydrate with only 1.4 g sugar, making it a moderate-energy, protein-rich meal option. Sodium is elevated at 314 mg per 100 g, primarily from the marinade, sauce, and any pickles. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore outlets. Clinically, the wrap has a medium glycaemic index (~57) driven by white pita bread, but the protein and fat content attenuate postprandial glucose response. It is suitable for most dietary patterns in moderate portions, with sodium management being the primary consideration for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients.

Can I eat shish tawook wrap if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Shish Tawook Wrap as a composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated using a weighted-component method anchored exclusively to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for the individual components (grilled chicken breast FDC ID 171477, white pita bread FDC ID 174924, tomato FDC ID 170457, cucumber FDC ID 168409, plain whole-milk yogurt FDC ID 171284), cross-referenced against the input record values (GrabFood provenance, confidence 0.7). The input values fall within plausible ranges for this dish type. GI is an analogous estimate based on white pita bread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite wrap dish is not available as a single composite entry in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-17. Component-level USDA data for grilled chicken breast does report MUFA and PUFA values, but these cannot be reliably aggregated to the full wrap without introducing unacceptable estimation error given variable sauce and marinade compositions. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values for the full composite wrap are not available as a single approved-source composite entry; USDA FoodData Central component data exists for individual ingredients but reliable composite aggregation is not possible within the approved methodology as of 2026-04-17. 3. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was listed under HPB Singapore but does not constitute a direct nutrient composition entry for this dish; it was treated as a menu listing reference only and was not used as a nutrient evidence source. All nutrient values are anchored to USDA FoodData Central approved entries. 4. Sodium at 314 mg/100g is borderline-elevated; a full 350 g serving delivers approximately 1,100 mg sodium. 5. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on white pita bread as the dominant carbohydrate source per the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008); actual GI of the composite wrap may be lower due to protein and fat attenuation. 6. The dish is classified as Turkish cuisine served in a Singapore food delivery context; multilingual names are provided for the Singapore market (Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per Project Veritas multilingual rules for Singapore-served dishes. Turkish romanized form (Shish Tavuk Dürüm) is included as a romanized alias.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Shish Tawook Wrap', a precise canonical English name. Local-language names provided for Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singapore-market multilingual rules. Turkish romanized form included.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (165.71), protein_g (10.86), fat_g (8.0), and carbohydrate_g (12.86) are all present, numeric, and non-zero. nutrient_source documents USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods as the approved anchor with explicit FDC IDs (171477, 174924, 170457, 168409, 171284) and a documented weighted-component estimation methodology. No forbidden sources used.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.14), sodium_mg (314.29), saturated_fat_g (2.0), and sugar_g (1.43) are all present as numeric values and are plausible for this dish type per USDA component data.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (57), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (25.8), and gi_source are all present. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database via analogous estimate (white pita bread GI 57, Atkinson et al. Diabetes Care 2008). Estimation basis fully documented in gi_source.source_match_notes. No forbidden GI sources used.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (wrap sandwich food product) used as closest_parent with gap note. FoodEx2 A0FJP (grilled chicken) used as closest_parent with gap note explaining composite dish limitation. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is approximately 1,250 characters, well above the 200-character minimum. Covers dish identity, preparation method, flavour and texture profile, cultural context, nutritional profile, clinical relevance, and serving context. common_user_queries, meal_context, and ingredient_synonyms are fully populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate, sodium-focused with specific mg figures), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate, GI and carbohydrate management with GL figure), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate, saturated fat and sodium with AHA reference). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly with machine key 'type2_diabetes'. Rheumatoid Arthritis included as additional_condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases flat array contains 16 entries covering alternate English spellings (Shish Taouk, Shish Tavuk), Chinese script forms (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, Turkish romanized forms, and descriptive English variants. aliases_by_language is fully populated. Self-check performed: all string values in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array — no discrepancies found.
